This rule involves identifying the penultimate (second-to-last) syllable of the root and forming the appended segment 'X' by taking the substring of the root starting from the first consonant of this penultimate syllable until the end of the word. Segmenting 'djemavac' into approximate syllables gives 'dje.ma.vac'. The penultimate syllable is 'ma'. Its first consonant is 'm'. Thus, `X = mavac`.
